199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

699	    def to_sampling_params(
700	        self,
701	        stop: List[str],
702	        model_generation_config: Dict[str, Any],
703	        tool_call_constraint: Optional[ToolCallConstraint] = None,
704	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
705	        """
706	        Convert request to sampling parameters.
707	        Priority: user value > model generation_config > OpenAI defaults
708	        """
709	
710	        def get_param(param_name: str):
711	            value = getattr(self, param_name)
712	            if value is None:
713	                return model_generation_config.get(
714	                    param_name, self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS[param_name]
715	                )
716	            return value
717	
718	        # add per user request
719	        spaces_between_special_tokens = (
720	            True
721	            if self.chat_template_kwargs is None
722	            else self.chat_template_kwargs.get("spaces_between_special_tokens", True)
723	        )
724	
725	        sampling_params = {
726	            "temperature": get_param("temperature"),
727	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_completion_tokens or self.max_tokens,
728	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_tokens,
729	            "stop": stop,
730	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
731	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
732	            "top_p": get_param("top_p"),
733	            "top_k": get_param("top_k"),
734	            "min_p": get_param("min_p"),
735	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
736	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
737	            "repetition_penalty": get_param("repetition_penalty"),
738	            "regex": self.regex,
739	            "ebnf": self.ebnf,
740	            "n": self.n,
741	            "no_stop_trim": self.no_stop_trim,
742	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
743	            "skip_special_tokens": self.skip_special_tokens,
744	            "logit_bias": self.logit_bias,
745	            "custom_params": self.custom_params,
746	            "sampling_seed": self.seed,
747	            "spaces_between_special_tokens": spaces_between_special_tokens,
748	        }
749	
750	        if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
751	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
752	                self.response_format.json_schema.schema_
753	            )
754	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
755	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
756	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
757	            sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
758	                self.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
759	            )
760	
761	        # Check if there are already existing output constraints
762	        has_existing_constraints = (
763	            sampling_params.get("regex")
764	            or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
765	            or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
766	            or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
767	        )
768	
769	        if tool_call_constraint and has_existing_constraints:
770	            logger.warning("Constrained decoding is not compatible with tool calls.")
771	        elif tool_call_constraint:
772	            constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
773	            if constraint_type == "structural_tag":
774	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
775	                    constraint_value.model_dump(by_alias=True)
776	                )
777	            elif constraint_type == "json_schema":
778	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
779	                    constraint_value  # type: ignore
780	                )
781	            else:
782	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = constraint_value
783	
784	        return sampling_params

156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar

181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

3	import dataclasses
4	import logging
5	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

17	
18	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

20	
21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass
189	
190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass
193	
194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None
201	            return
202	
203	        # Find a grammar from the list
204	        first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
205	
206	        # TODO(lianmin): Maybe we can reuse the existing mask?
207	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
208	            vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
209	            batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
210	            device=self.device,
211	        )
212	        self.apply_mask_func = (
213	            first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
214	        )  # force to use static method
215	
216	        # Apply the mask
217	        for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
218	            if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
219	                grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
220	
221	        # Move the mask to the device if needed
222	        self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
223	
224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234

236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:

239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)

40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78

80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
86	
87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )
92

94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()
95	        if ff_tokens:
96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None

101	        return "", -1

105	    ):

109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

650	async def generate_request(obj: GenerateReqInput, request: Request):
651	    """Handle a generate request."""
652	    if obj.stream:
653	
654	        async def stream_results() -> AsyncIterator[bytes]:
655	            try:
656	                async for out in _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
657	                    obj, request
658	                ):
659	                    yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
660	                        out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
661	                    ) + b"\n\n"
662	            except ValueError as e:
663	                out = {"error": {"message": str(e)}}
664	                logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
665	                yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
666	                    out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
667	                ) + b"\n\n"
668	            yield b"data: [DONE]\n\n"
669	
670	        return StreamingResponse(

674	        )

186	    def get_structure_constraint(
187	        self, tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["auto", "required"]]
188	    ) -> Optional[ToolCallConstraint]:
189	        """
190	        Returns the appropriate structure constraint for tool calls based on the tool_choice.
191	        The constraint is used to guide the model's output format.
192	
193	        Args:
194	            tool_choice: The tool choice setting from the request
195	
196	        Returns:
197	            A tuple of (constraint_type, constraint_value) to be added to sampling parameters,
198	            or None if no constraint applies.
199	        """
200	        # NOTE: structural_tag only supports JSON-compatible content between the begin and end.
201	        # It cannot parse or validate function call Pythonic or XML-ish syntax.
202	        if (
203	            self.detector.supports_structural_tag()
204	            and tool_choice == "auto"
205	            and (
206	                any(tool.function.strict for tool in self.tools)
207	                or self.tool_strict_level >= ToolStrictLevel.FUNCTION
208	            )
209	        ):
210	            tag = self.get_structure_tag()
211	            return ("structural_tag", tag)
212	        elif tool_choice == "required" or isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
213	            json_schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, tool_choice)
214	            return ("json_schema", json_schema)

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for reasoner grammar-guided constrained decoding."""
15	
16	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
17	
18	import torch
19	
20	from .base_grammar_backend import (
21	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
22	    BaseGrammarBackend,
23	    BaseGrammarObject,
24	)
25	
26	
27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )
107	
108	
109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj
125

69	class RequestTimingMetricsMixin:

82	    # In different modes:
83	    #   - Unified/PD-colocate: timestamp when forward computation begins (covers prefill + decode)
84	    #   - Prefill instance (P): timestamp when prefill forward pass begins
85	    #   - Decode instance (D): timestamp when decode forward pass begins
86	    # Note: This is NOT the same as prefill_start_time. There may be a delay between
87	    # forward_entry_time and prefill_start_time (see prefill_launch_delay).
88	    forward_entry_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
89

1	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
2	from dataclasses import dataclass
3	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
4	
5	import numpy as np
6	
7	
8	@dataclass
9	class ChoicesDecision:
10	    decision: str
11	    meta_info: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
12	
13	
14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...
30	
31	
32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
51	
52	
53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()
54	
55	
56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()
108	
109	
110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()

29	        self.tools = [
30	            Tool(
31	                type="function",
32	                function=Function(

34	                    description="Get weather information",
35	                    parameters={
36	                        "type": "object",
37	                        "properties": {
38	                            "location": {
39	                                "type": "string",
40	                                "description": "Location to get weather for",
41	                            },
42	                            "unit": {

45	                                "description": "Temperature unit",
46	                            },
47	                        },

49	                    },

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Comprehensive SGLang Constraint Implementation Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the SGLang constraint system. Here's what I found:

### 1. **JSON-Related Constraints**

**Primary Files:**
- `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` - Contains JSON schema constraint generation and tool schema handling
- `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py` - Comprehensive test suite

**Key Implementations:**
- `get_json_schema_constraint()` - Converts OpenAI tool definitions into JSON schema constraints
- `_get_tool_schema_defs()` - Consolidates and validates $defs across multiple tools
- `infer_type_from_json_schema()` - Infers JSON types from schema definitions
- Support for JSON $defs merging with conflict detection

### 2. **Constraint Architecture - Grammar Manager & Backends**

**Core System Architecture:**
- `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` - Base interface for all grammar backends
- `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` - Manages constraint processing and caching

**Base Architecture Pattern:**
```
BaseGrammarBackend
├── dispatch_json(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
├── dispatch_regex(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
├── dispatch_ebnf(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
├── dispatch_structural_tag(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
├── dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
└── dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
```

**Grammar Manager Flow:**
1. Request arrives with constraint (json_schema, regex, ebnf, or structural_tag)
2. GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar() creates cache key: `(type, constraint_string)`
3. Checks cache via backend.get_cached_or_future_value()
4. If cache miss, submits to ThreadPoolExecutor for compilation
5. Polling mechanism waits for grammar compilation to complete
6. Compiled grammar becomes BaseGrammarObject with token mask generation methods

### 3. **Backend Implementations**

**Three Primary Backends:**

**A. XGrammar Backend** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`)
- Uses `xgrammar==0.1.27` library
- Implements: JSON schema, regex, EBNF, structural tags
- Key Class: `XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject)`
- Features: Token bitmask allocation, vocab mask filling, token rollback (up to 200 tokens)
- Grammar compilation methods:
  - `compile_builtin_json_grammar()` - For `$$ANY$$` builtin JSON
  - `compile_json_schema(schema, any_whitespace)` - Schema to grammar
  - `compile_regex(regex_string)`
  - `compile_grammar(ebnf_string)`
  - `compile_structural_tag(tags_list, triggers)`

**B. Outlines Backend** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`)
- Uses `outlines==0.1.11` library
- Implements: JSON schema, regex
- Key Class: `OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject)`
- Uses FSM-based approach with RegexGuide
- Method: `build_regex_from_schema(schema_json)` converts JSON schema to regex

**C. LLGuidance Backend** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py`)
- Uses `llguidance>=0.7.11,<0.8.0` library
- Implements: JSON schema, regex, EBNF, structural tags
- Key Class: `GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject)`
- Methods:
  - `LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(schema_json, defaults)`
  - `grammar_from("regex", pattern)`
  - `grammar_from("ebnf", grammar_string)`

**D. Reasoner Grammar Wrapper** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/reasoner_grammar_backend.py`)
- Wraps any backend to support CoT reasoning
- Handles think_end_id token to separate reasoning from output
- Key Class: `ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject)`

### 4. **Constraint-to-Grammar/FSM Conversion Flow**

**Request Processing Pipeline:**
```
CompletionRequest (Protocol)
  ↓
[json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag, response_format]
  ↓
SamplingParams
  ↓
GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
  ↓
Backend.dispatch_[json|regex|ebnf|structural_tag]()
  ↓
Grammar Compiler (XGrammar/Outlines/LLGuidance)
  ↓
Compiled Grammar Object
  ↓
TokenMask Generation (fill_vocab_mask)
  ↓
Sampler (applies mask to logits)
```

**Vocab Mask Application:**
- `BaseGrammarObject.allocate_vocab_mask()` - Creates boolean tensor (batch_size × vocab_size)
- `fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, batch_idx)` - Marks allowed tokens as True
- `apply_vocab_mask(logits, vocab_mask)` - Sets invalid tokens to -inf

### 5. **Server-Side Constraint Processing**

**Protocol Integration** (`/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`):
- CompletionRequest fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- ChatCompletionRequest supports `response_format` (json_schema, json_object, structural_tag)
- `to_sampling_params()` method converts request → SamplingParams

**Sampling Parameters** (`/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`):
- Stores constraint strings: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- No dtype constraint (not yet implemented)

**Server Args** (`/python/sglang/srt/server_args.py`):
- `--grammar-backend` option: "xgrammar", "outlines", "llguidance", "none"
- `--constrained-json-whitespace-pattern` - Custom whitespace handling
- `--constrained-json-disable-any-whitespace` - Strict whitespace mode

### 6. **Dependencies & External Libraries**

**Key Dependencies** (from `/python/pyproject.toml`):
- `xgrammar==0.1.27` - FSM-based grammar compilation
- `outlines==0.1.11` - Regex/JSON schema via FSM
- `llguidance>=0.7.11,<0.8.0` - Advanced constraint guidance
- `interegular` - Regex analysis (used by Outlines)
- `partial_json_parser` - Streaming JSON validation
- `orjson` - Fast JSON operations
- `jsonschema` - Schema validation (for tests)

### 7. **Validation & Enforcement Patterns**

**Grammar Objects Expose Standard Interface:**
```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(token: int) → None       # Token accepted by grammar
    def fill_vocab_mask(mask, idx) → None     # Mark valid tokens
    def is_terminated() → bool                 # Grammar complete
    def rollback(k: int) → None                # Undo k tokens
    def try_jump_forward(tokenizer) → Optional[Tuple]  # Optimization
    def copy() → BaseGrammarObject             # Per-request instance
```

**Validation Happens In:**
- Grammar compilation phase (catches invalid schema/regex early)
- Token-by-token in sampler (invalid tokens masked to -inf)
- Rollback mechanism for spec decoding compatibility

### 8. **No JSONPath/Dtype Constraints Found**

- **No jsonpath constraint** exists in current codebase
- **No dtype constraint** - Only general type inference from JSON schemas
- **No dot-notation path constraints** for nested field validation

### 9. **Extension Points for New Constraints**

To add a new constraint type (e.g., jsonpath):

1. **Add dispatch method** in `BaseGrammarBackend`:
   ```python
   def dispatch_jsonpath(self, key_string: str) → Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
   ```

2. **Implement in each backend** (XGrammar, Outlines, LLGuidance)

3. **Add to grammar_manager** cache key types in `grammar_manager.py`:
   ```python
   if req.sampling_params.jsonpath is not None:
       key = ("jsonpath", req.sampling_params.jsonpath)
   ```

4. **Add to SamplingParams** (`sampling_params.py`):
   ```python
   jsonpath: Optional[str] = None
   ```

5. **Add to protocol** (`protocol.py`):
   ```python
   jsonpath: Optional[str] = None
   ```

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Lines |
|------|---------|-------|
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Base interface & dispatch pattern | 275 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Request queuing & caching | 196 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | XGrammar integration | 357 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | Outlines integration | 191 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | LLGuidance integration | 179 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` | JSON schema utilities | 247 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Parameter definitions | ~300 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | API protocol | ~800+ |

**Sampling Parameters** (`/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`):
- Stores constraint strings: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`
- No dtype constraint (not yet implemented)

**Server Args** (`/python/sglang/srt/server_args.py`):
- `--grammar-backend` option: "xgrammar", "outlines", "llguidance", "none"
- `--constrained-json-whitespace-pattern` - Custom whitespace handling
- `--constrained-json-disable-any-whitespace` - Strict whitespace mode

### 6. **Dependencies & External Libraries**

**Key Dependencies** (from `/python/pyproject.toml`):
- `xgrammar==0.1.27` - FSM-based grammar compilation
- `outlines==0.1.11` - Regex/JSON schema via FSM
- `llguidance>=0.7.11,<0.8.0` - Advanced constraint guidance
- `interegular` - Regex analysis (used by Outlines)
- `partial_json_parser` - Streaming JSON validation
- `orjson` - Fast JSON operations
- `jsonschema` - Schema validation (for tests)

### 7. **Validation & Enforcement Patterns**

**Grammar Objects Expose Standard Interface:**
```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(token: int) → None       # Token accepted by grammar
    def fill_vocab_mask(mask, idx) → None     # Mark valid tokens
    def is_terminated() → bool                 # Grammar complete
    def rollback(k: int) → None                # Undo k tokens
    def try_jump_forward(tokenizer) → Optional[Tuple]  # Optimization
    def copy() → BaseGrammarObject             # Per-request instance
```

**Validation Happens In:**
- Grammar compilation phase (catches invalid schema/regex early)
- Token-by-token in sampler (invalid tokens masked to -inf)
- Rollback mechanism for spec decoding compatibility

### 8. **No JSONPath/Dtype Constraints Found**

- **No jsonpath constraint** exists in current codebase
- **No dtype constraint** - Only general type inference from JSON schemas
- **No dot-notation path constraints** for nested field validation

### 9. **Extension Points for New Constraints**

To add a new constraint type (e.g., jsonpath):

1. **Add dispatch method** in `BaseGrammarBackend`:
   ```python
   def dispatch_jsonpath(self, key_string: str) → Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
   ```

2. **Implement in each backend** (XGrammar, Outlines, LLGuidance)

3. **Add to grammar_manager** cache key types in `grammar_manager.py`:
   ```python
   if req.sampling_params.jsonpath is not None:
       key = ("jsonpath", req.sampling_params.jsonpath)
   ```

4. **Add to SamplingParams** (`sampling_params.py`):
   ```python
   jsonpath: Optional[str] = None
   ```

5. **Add to protocol** (`protocol.py`):
   ```python
   jsonpath: Optional[str] = None
   ```

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Lines |
|------|---------|-------|
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Base interface & dispatch pattern | 275 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Request queuing & caching | 196 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | XGrammar integration | 357 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | Outlines integration | 191 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | LLGuidance integration | 179 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` | JSON schema utilities | 247 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Parameter definitions | ~300 |
| `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | API protocol | ~800+ |

## Summary: SGLang Output Constraint System

I've completed a thorough exploration of SGLang's output constraint system. Here's the comprehensive breakdown:

### 1. **Core Architecture Files**

#### **Language Frontend (IR & API)**
- **Path**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504087327-1f8830/python/sglang/lang/`
  - `api.py` - Public constraint APIs (`gen()`, `gen_int()`, `gen_string()`, `select()`)
  - `ir.py` - Intermediate representation with `SglGen`, `SglSelect`, `SglSamplingParams` classes
  - `choices.py` - Choice sampling method implementations

#### **Sampling Parameters**
- **Path**: `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`
  - **Class**: `SamplingParams`
  - **Constraint Fields** (lines 55-58):
    - `json_schema: Optional[str]` - JSON schema for structured output
    - `regex: Optional[str]` - Regular expression constraint
    - `ebnf: Optional[str]` - EBNF grammar constraint
    - `structural_tag: Optional[str]` - Structural tag for function calling

### 2. **Constraint Definition Classes**

#### **SglSamplingParams (Language Frontend)**
- **Location**: `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` lines 17-62
- **Key Fields**:
  - `regex: Optional[str]` - User-provided regex pattern
  - `json_schema: Optional[str]` - User-provided JSON schema
  - Includes conversion methods: `to_openai_kwargs()`, `to_srt_kwargs()`, etc.

#### **SglGen (Generation Node)**
- **Location**: `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` lines 451-504
- **Constructor Parameters**:
  - `regex: Optional[str]`
  - `json_schema: Optional[str]`
  - Wraps all constraint parameters in `SglSamplingParams`

#### **SglSelect (Choice Constraint)**
- **Location**: `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` lines 533-550
- **Constructor Parameters**:
  - `choices: List[str]` - List of choice options
  - `temperature: float` - Sampling temperature
  - `choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod` - Selection strategy

### 3. **Grammar Backend System (Constrained Decoding)**

#### **Base Backend Class**
- **Path**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`
- **Class**: `BaseGrammarBackend`
- **Key Methods**:
  ```python
  def dispatch_json(key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
  def dispatch_regex(key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
  def dispatch_ebnf(key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
  def dispatch_structural_tag(key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]
  def dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string: str)  # Placeholder
  def dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)    # Placeholder
  ```
- **Dispatch Router** (lines 157-179):
  - `_init_value_dispatch()` routes based on `(key_type, key_string)` tuple
  - **Key Types**: `"json"`, `"regex"`, `"ebnf"`, `"structural_tag"`, `"structural_pattern"`, `"structural_pattern_v2"`

#### **Grammar Objects**
- **Base Class**: `BaseGrammarObject` (lines 42-118)
- **Key Methods**:
  - `accept_token(token: int)` - Consume tokens one at a time
  - `fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)` - Restrict vocabulary at each step
  - `allocate_vocab_mask()` - Create vocab constraints
  - `try_jump_forward()` - Optimization for long constant sequences

### 4. **Concrete Backend Implementations**

#### **OutlinesGrammarBackend**
- **Path**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`
- **Grammar Class**: `OutlinesGrammar`
- **Supported Constraints**:
  - `dispatch_json()` - Lines 166-175
  - `dispatch_regex()` - Lines 177-178
  - `dispatch_ebnf()` - Lines 160-161 (delegates to parent)
  - `dispatch_structural_tag()` - Lines 163-164 (delegates to parent)
- **Dependencies**: `outlines` library with `RegexGuide`, `build_regex_from_schema()`

#### **XGrammarGrammarBackend**
- **Path**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` lines 175+
- **Grammar Class**: `XGrammarGrammar`
- **Supported Constraints**:
  - `dispatch_json()` - Lines 257-270
  - `dispatch_regex()` - Lines 280-286
  - `dispatch_ebnf()` - Lines 272-278
  - `dispatch_structural_tag()` - Lines 288-317 (with legacy format support)
- **Dependencies**: `xgrammar` library with `GrammarCompiler`, `GrammarMatcher`
- **Special Methods**:
  - `_sanitize_structural_format()` - Validates/normalizes constraint structures
  - `_sanitize_structural_tag_structures()` - Handles legacy structural tag format

#### **GuidanceBackend (llguidance)**
- **Path**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py`
- **Grammar Class**: `GuidanceGrammar`
- **Supported Constraints**:
  - `dispatch_json()` - Lines 135-147
  - `dispatch_regex()` - Lines 149-151
  - `dispatch_ebnf()` - Lines 153-159
  - `dispatch_structural_tag()` - Lines 161-178
- **Dependencies**: `llguidance` library

#### **ReasonerGrammarBackend (Wrapper)**
- **Path**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/reasoner_grammar_backend.py`
- **Purpose**: Wraps another backend to support constraint generation with reasoning tokens
- **Class**: `ReasonerGrammarObject` - Tracks `think_end_id` token
- **Behavior**: Only applies constraints after thinking concludes

### 5. **Registration & Factory Pattern**

#### **Backend Registration**
- **Location**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` lines 199-274
- **Registry**: `GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY` dict (line 199)
- **Registration Function**: `register_grammar_backend(name, init_func)` (lines 202-203)
- **Factory Function**: `create_grammar_backend()` (lines 206-274)

#### **Backend Selection Logic** (lines 212-263):
```python
if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:  # Custom backends have priority
    return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](...)
elif name == "outlines":
    return OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
elif name == "xgrammar":
    return XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
elif name == "llguidance":
    return GuidanceBackend(...)
elif name == "none":
    return None
```

#### **Command-line Argument**:
- `--grammar-backend` (values: `"outlines"`, `"xgrammar"`, `"llguidance"`, `"none"`)

### 6. **Request Processing Pipeline**

#### **GrammarManager**
- **Path**: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`
- **Key Method**: `process_req_with_grammar()` (lines 67-105)
- **Constraint Detection** (lines 71-87):
  ```python
  if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
      key = ("json", json_schema_string)
  elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
      key = ("regex", regex_string)
  elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
      key = ("ebnf", ebnf_string)
  elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
      key = ("structural_tag", tag_string)
  ```

### 7. **Public API Functions**

#### **Language Frontend APIs** (`python/sglang/lang/api.py`)
- **`gen()`** - Lines 75-139: Generic generation with all constraint types
  - Parameters: `regex`, `json_schema`, `choices`, `choices_method`
  - Returns: `SglSelect` for choices, `SglGen` for others
  
- **`select()`** - Lines 236-243: Choice constraint wrapper
  - Parameters: `choices`, `choices_method` (default: `token_length_normalized`)
  
- **`gen_int()` / `gen_string()`** - Lines 142-225: Type-specific generation

#### **Choice Selection Methods** (`python/sglang/lang/choices.py`)
- `TokenLengthNormalized` - Highest token-length-normalized logprob
- `GreedyTokenSelection` - Greedy token-by-token selection
- `UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized` - Requires unconditional logprobs

### 8. **OpenAI-Compatible Protocol**

#### **Constraint Types in Protocol** (`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`)
- **CompletionRequest** fields (lines 268-270):
  - `json_schema: Optional[str]`
  - `regex: Optional[str]`
  - `ebnf: Optional[str]`
  
- **Response Formats**:
  - `JsonSchemaResponseFormat` (lines 154-155)
  - `StructuralTagResponseFormat` (lines 166-174)

### 9. **Constraint Flow**

```
User Program → SglGen (with constraint) 
    ↓
IR Compilation → SglSamplingParams
    ↓
Language Backend Execution
    ↓
SamplingParams (in srt/) with constraint field
    ↓
GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
    ↓
Backend Selection (outlines/xgrammar/llguidance)
    ↓
Grammar Compilation (dispatch_json/regex/ebnf/structural_tag)
    ↓
Token Generation with Vocab Masking
    ↓
Output respects constraint (validated JSON/regex/EBNF)
```

### 10. **Test Files**

- **Constraint Tests**: `test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py`
- **Test Kits**:
  - `python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`
  - `python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py`
  - `python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py`
- **Function Call Tests**: `test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`

### 11. **Key Design Patterns**

1. **Dispatch Pattern**: Multiple dispatch methods for different constraint types
2. **Backend Abstraction**: Pluggable backends (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance)
3. **Caching**: Grammar objects cached by `(key_type, key_string)` tuple to avoid recompilation
4. **Async Compilation**: Grammar compilation in thread pool to avoid blocking
5. **Vocab Masking**: Constraints implemented as vocabulary restrictions per token
6. **Delegation**: ReasonerGrammarBackend wraps other backends for composition

### 11. **Key Design Patterns**

1. **Dispatch Pattern**: Multiple dispatch methods for different constraint types
2. **Backend Abstraction**: Pluggable backends (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance)
3. **Caching**: Grammar objects cached by `(key_type, key_string)` tuple to avoid recompilation

```python
def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    if sampling_params.dtype is None:
        return

    dtype_regex = None
    if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_INT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT  # r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
        sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_STR  # r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""
    elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
        dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL  # r"(True|False)"
    
    if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
        warnings.warn("Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used.")
    
    sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
```

- `generate_stream()` (lines 198-246): Streaming generation

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if self.grammar_backend is None:
            error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (...) is not supported..."
        else:
            if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
            elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
            elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
            elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
            
            value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                key, req.require_reasoning
            )
            req.grammar = value
```

**Grammar Backends Available:**

- `outlines_backend.py`: Outlines backend

```python
def _init_value_dispatch(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool):
    key_type, key_string = key
    if key_type == "json":
        grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
    elif key_type == "regex":
        grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
    elif key_type == "ebnf":
        grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
    elif key_type == "structural_tag":
        grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
```

Each backend (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance) implements these dispatch methods to compile constraints into grammar objects.

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504087327-1f8830/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`

**Key Method:** `update_regex_vocab_mask()` (lines 197-222)

Creates vocabulary masks for constrained generation:

```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    if not self.grammars:
        self.vocab_mask = None
        self.apply_mask_func = None
        return

    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
    
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device,
    )
    self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
    
    # Apply the mask
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
```

**Constraint Application:** `apply_logits_bias()` (lines 235-248)

```python
def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    # ... penalties ...
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
    # ... logit_bias ...
```

The vocabulary mask zeros out logits of disallowed tokens based on grammar constraints.

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504087327-1f8830/examples/frontend_language/usage/json_decode.py`

```python
    s += "Please fill in the following information about this character.\n"
    s += sgl.gen(
        "json_output", 
        max_tokens=256, 
        regex=character_regex  # Pass regex constraint
    )

# Usage
state = character_gen.run(name="Hermione Granger")
```

### COMPLETE FLOW SUMMARY

1. **User calls `sgl.gen(..., regex=..., json_schema=..., dtype=...)`** in a function
2. **API layer (api.py)** validates regex and creates `SglGen` IR object with constraints
3. **SglGen wraps constraints** in `SglSamplingParams` dataclass
4. **Interpreter (_execute_gen)** passes `SglSamplingParams` to backend
5. **Runtime endpoint (backend adapter)** converts dtype to regex if needed
6. **HTTP request** serializes constraints in `sampling_params` dict
7. **Grammar manager** checks for constraints and creates grammar objects
8. **Grammar backends** (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) compile constraints into state machines
9. **Sampling batch info** converts grammar to vocabulary masks
10. **Sampling kernel** applies masks to logits to enforce constraints

---

### KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | User API: `gen()` function definition |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | IR classes: `SglGen`, `SglSamplingParams` |
| `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | Execution: `_execute_gen()` |
| `python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` | Backend adapter: dtype->regex conversion |
| `python/sglang/srt/managers/io_struct.py` | HTTP request structure: `GenerateReqInput` |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Backend params: validation & parsing |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Grammar coordination & caching |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Grammar dispatch & compilation |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` | Batch constraint application via masks |

Checks if a request has constraints and creates grammar objects:

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if self.grammar_backend is None:
            error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (...) is not supported..."
        else:
            if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
            elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
            elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
            elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
            
            value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                key, req.require_reasoning
            )
            req.grammar = value
```

**Grammar Backends Available:**
- `xgrammar_backend.py`: XGrammar backend
- `outlines_backend.py`: Outlines backend
- `llguidance_backend.py`: LLGuidance backend

#### 6c. Grammar Backend Base Class

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504087327-1f8830/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`

**BaseGrammarBackend Class** (lines 129-274) provides dispatch methods:

```python
def _init_value_dispatch(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool):
    key_type, key_string = key
    if key_type == "json":
        grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
    elif key_type == "regex":
        grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
    elif key_type == "ebnf":
        grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
    elif key_type == "structural_tag":
        grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
```

Each backend (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance) implements these dispatch methods to compile constraints into grammar objects.

---

### 7. SAMPLING BATCH INFO - CONSTRAINT APPLICATION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504087327-1f8830/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`

**Key Method:** `update_regex_vocab_mask()` (lines 197-222)

Creates vocabulary masks for constrained generation:

```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    if not self.grammars:
        self.vocab_mask = None
        self.apply_mask_func = None
        return

    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
    
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device,
    )
    self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
    
    # Apply the mask
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)
```

**Constraint Application:** `apply_logits_bias()` (lines 235-248)

```python
def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    # ... penalties ...
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
    # ... logit_bias ...
```

The vocabulary mask zeros out logits of disallowed tokens based on grammar constraints.

---

### 8. USER EXAMPLE

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504087327-1f8830/examples/frontend_language/usage/json_decode.py`

```python
@sgl.function
def character_gen(s, name):
    s += "Please fill in the following information about this character.\n"
    s += sgl.gen(
        "json_output", 
        max_tokens=256, 
        regex=character_regex  # Pass regex constraint
    )

# Usage
state = character_gen.run(name="Hermione Granger")
```

---

### COMPLETE FLOW SUMMARY

1. **User calls `sgl.gen(..., regex=..., json_schema=..., dtype=...)`** in a function
2. **API layer (api.py)** validates regex and creates `SglGen` IR object with constraints
3. **SglGen wraps constraints** in `SglSamplingParams` dataclass
4. **Interpreter (_execute_gen)** passes `SglSamplingParams` to backend
5. **Runtime endpoint (backend adapter)** converts dtype to regex if needed
6. **HTTP request** serializes constraints in `sampling_params` dict
7. **Grammar manager** checks for constraints and creates grammar objects
8. **Grammar backends** (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) compile constraints into state machines
9. **Sampling batch info** converts grammar to vocabulary masks
10. **Sampling kernel** applies masks to logits to enforce constraints

---

### KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | User API: `gen()` function definition |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | IR classes: `SglGen`, `SglSamplingParams` |
| `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | Execution: `_execute_gen()` |
| `python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` | Backend adapter: dtype->regex conversion |
| `python/sglang/srt/managers/io_struct.py` | HTTP request structure: `GenerateReqInput` |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Backend params: validation & parsing |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Grammar coordination & caching |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Grammar dispatch & compilation |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` | Batch constraint application via masks |

Excellent! I now have a very thorough understanding of the SGLang constraint system. Let me read the key files to get exact line numbers and code details before creating my plan.

The key insight is that existing constraints (json_schema, regex, ebnf) work by constraining token generation at the grammar/FSM level - they restrict which tokens can be generated at each step. A "json_path" constraint is fundamentally different: it validates that the *final output* contains a specific JSON path pattern. 

There are two possible approaches:
1. **Post-generation validation** - Generate valid JSON (using json_schema constraint) and then validate the output contains the specified path


2. **Grammar-based constraint** - Convert the JSON path requirement into a JSON schema that ensures the path exists, then use the existing json_schema constraint

Approach 2 is much more elegant because it leverages the existing constrained decoding infrastructure. A JSON path like `$.store.book[*].author` can be converted to a JSON schema that requires `store.book` to be an array of objects with `author` fields. This ensures the path exists during generation rather than validating it afterward. The challenge is handling the full complexity of JSON path syntax - wildcards, filters, and nested structures all need to map correctly to schema constraints.

The pattern mirrors how `dtype` works by converting to regex and using existing infrastructure. I'd write a converter function that transforms paths like `$.store.book[*].author` into schemas requiring `{"store": {"book": [{"author": ...}]}}`, then leverage the json_schema constraint system we already have. Now I need to check the actual codebase to map out where these changes would go.

170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))

206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total
