14	"""Constrained decoding with xgrammar backend."""
15	
16	import dataclasses
17	import json
18	import logging
19	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union
20	
21	import torch
22	from xgrammar import (
23	    CompiledGrammar,
24	    GrammarCompiler,
25	    GrammarMatcher,
26	    StructuralTagItem,
27	    TokenizerInfo,
28	    allocate_token_bitmask,
29	)
30	
31	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
32	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
33	    BaseGrammarBackend,
34	    BaseGrammarObject,
35	    GrammarStats,
36	)
37	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
38	from sglang.srt.utils import is_hip
39	
40	_is_hip = is_hip()
41	if _is_hip:
42	    from sgl_kernel import apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda
43	else:
44	    from sglang.srt.constrained.triton_ops.bitmask_ops import (
45	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton,
46	    )
47	
48	
49	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
50	MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS = 200
51	
52	
53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )
137	
138	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
139	        s = self.matcher.find_jump_forward_string()
140	        if s:
141	            return [], s
142	        return None
143	
144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167	
168	
169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}
239	
240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250	            self.vocab_size,
251	            ctx,
252	            self.override_stop_tokens,
253	            key_string,
254	            grammar_stats,
255	        )
256	
257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]
302	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(
303	                    tags, structural_tag["triggers"]
304	                )
305	            else:
306	                format_dict = structural_tag.get("format")
307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)
309	                    structural_tag["format"] = format_dict
310	                    key_string = json.dumps(structural_tag)
311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
313	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
314	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
315	        return self._from_context(
316	            ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag")
317	        )
318	
319	    def reset(self):
320	        self.grammar_compiler.clear_cache()
321	
322	
323	def demo_test():
324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer
325	
326	    from sglang.test.test_utils import DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST
327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
330	
331	    # Should use vocab size from model config
332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size
333	    eos_token_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354	
355	if __name__ == "__main__":
356	    demo_test()

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

8	class TestJSONConstrainedMixin:
9	    json_schema = json.dumps(
10	        {
11	            "type": "object",
12	            "properties": {
13	                "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
14	                "population": {"type": "integer"},
15	            },
16	            "required": ["name", "population"],
17	            "additionalProperties": False,
18	        }
19	    )
20	
21	    def _run_decode_json(
22	        self, json_schema, return_logprob=False, top_logprobs_num=0, n=1
23	    ):
24	        response = requests.post(
25	            self.base_url + "/generate",
26	            json={
27	                "text": (
28	                    "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. The JSON Schema is: "
29	                    + json.dumps(json_schema)
30	                ),
31	                "sampling_params": {
32	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
33	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
34	                    "n": n,
35	                    "stop_token_ids": [119690],
36	                    "json_schema": json_schema,
37	                },
38	                "stream": False,
39	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
40	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
41	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
42	            },
43	        )
44	        ret = response.json()
45	        print(json.dumps(ret))
46	        print("=" * 100)
47	
48	        if not json_schema or json_schema == "INVALID":
49	            return
50	
51	        # Make sure the json output is valid
52	        try:
53	            js_obj = json.loads(ret["text"])
54	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
55	            raise
56	
57	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
58	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
59	
60	    def test_json_generate(self):
61	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema=self.json_schema)
62	
63	    def test_json_invalid(self):
64	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema="INVALID")
65	
66	    def test_json_openai(self):
67	        client = openai.Client(api_key="EMPTY", base_url=f"{self.base_url}/v1")
68	
69	        response = client.chat.completions.create(
70	            model=self.model,
71	            messages=[
72	                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant"},
73	                {
74	                    "role": "user",
75	                    "content": "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. "
76	                    "The JSON Schema is: " + json.dumps(self.json_schema),
77	                },
78	            ],
79	            temperature=0,
80	            max_tokens=128,
81	            response_format={
82	                "type": "json_schema",
83	                "json_schema": {"name": "foo", "schema": json.loads(self.json_schema)},
84	            },
85	        )
86	        text = response.choices[0].message.content
87	
88	        try:
89	            js_obj = json.loads(text)
90	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
91	            print("JSONDecodeError", text)
92	            raise
93	
94	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
95	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
96	
97	    def test_mix_json_and_other(self):
98	        json_schemas = [None, None, self.json_schema, self.json_schema] * 10
99	
100	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(len(json_schemas)) as executor:

63	            temperature=0,

19	class TestJSONModeMixin:
20	    """Mixin class containing JSON mode test methods"""
21	
22	    def test_json_mode_response(self):
23	        """Test that response_format json_object (also known as "json mode") produces valid JSON, even without a system prompt that mentions JSON."""
24	        response = self.client.chat.completions.create(
25	            model=self.model,
26	            messages=[
27	                # We are deliberately omitting "That produces JSON" or similar phrases from the assistant prompt so that we don't have misleading test results
28	                {
29	                    "role": "system",
30	                    "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant that gives a short answer.",
31	                },
32	                {"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of Bulgaria?"},
33	            ],
34	            temperature=0,
35	            max_tokens=128,
36	            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
37	        )
38	        text = response.choices[0].message.content
39	
40	        print(f"Response ({len(text)} characters): {text}")
41	
42	        # Verify the response is valid JSON
43	        try:
44	            js_obj = json.loads(text)
45	        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
46	            self.fail(f"Response is not valid JSON. Error: {e}. Response: {text}")
47	
48	        # Verify it's actually an object (dict)
49	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj, dict, f"Response is not a JSON object: {text}")
50	
51	    def test_json_mode_with_streaming(self):
52	        """Test that streaming with json_object response (also known as "json mode") format works correctly, even without a system prompt that mentions JSON."""
53	        stream = self.client.chat.completions.create(
54	            model=self.model,
55	            messages=[
56	                # We are deliberately omitting "That produces JSON" or similar phrases from the assistant prompt so that we don't have misleading test results
57	                {
58	                    "role": "system",
59	                    "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant that gives a short answer.",
60	                },
61	                {"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of Bulgaria?"},
62	            ],
63	            temperature=0,
64	            max_tokens=128,
65	            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
66	            stream=True,
67	        )
68	
69	        # Collect all chunks
70	        chunks = []
71	        for chunk in stream:
72	            if chunk.choices[0].delta.content is not None:
73	                chunks.append(chunk.choices[0].delta.content)
74	        full_response = "".join(chunks)
75	
76	        print(
77	            f"Concatenated Response ({len(full_response)} characters): {full_response}"
78	        )
79	
80	        # Verify the combined response is valid JSON
81	        try:
82	            js_obj = json.loads(full_response)
83	        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
84	            self.fail(
85	                f"Streamed response is not valid JSON. Error: {e}. Response: {full_response}"
86	            )
87	
88	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj, dict)

Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings.

## Summary

I've thoroughly explored the SGLang codebase to locate where HTTP API request/response schemas are defined and how constrained generation parameters are handled. Here are my findings:

### **PRIMARY FILES - HTTP API Request/Response Schemas**

**1. /absolute/path/to/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py** (Main Protocol Definitions)
   - **CompletionRequest** (lines 236-309): Defines HTTP request schema for completions endpoint
     - Fields: `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf` (lines 268-270)
     - Also has `response_format` field (line 279) for response-based constraints
   
   - **ChatCompletionRequest** (lines 529-627): Defines HTTP request schema for chat completions endpoint
     - Fields: `regex`, `ebnf` (lines 580-581)
     - Also has `response_format` field (line 553) for response-based constraints
     - **Note**: `ChatCompletionRequest` does NOT have direct `json_schema` field; instead uses `response_format.type == "json_schema"`
   
   - **ResponseFormat** (lines 152-155): Maps `type: "json_schema"` to constrained generation
   - **LegacyStructuralTagResponseFormat** (lines 164-168): Maps `type: "structural_tag"` to constrained generation
   - **StructuralTagResponseFormat** (lines 170-172): Type alias for structural tag support
   
   - **Key Conversion Methods**:
     - `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` (lines 699-784): Converts HTTP request fields to `SamplingParams`
       - Handles json_schema via `response_format.type == "json_schema"` (lines 750-753)
       - Handles json_object via `response_format.type == "json_object"` (lines 754-755)
       - Handles structural_tag via `response_format.type == "structural_tag"` (lines 756-759)
       - Directly maps `regex` and `ebnf` to sampling_params (lines 738-739)
     
     - Utility function `convert_json_schema_to_str()` from `sglang.utils` (line 45): Converts JSON schema dicts to strings

**2. /absolute/path/to/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py** (Sampling Parameters)
   - **SamplingParams class** (lines 31-96): Core class that accepts constrained generation parameters
     - Constructor parameters (lines 40-67):
       - `json_schema: Optional[str] = None` (line 55)
       - `regex: Optional[str] = None` (line 56)
       - `ebnf: Optional[str] = None` (line 57)
       - `structural_tag: Optional[str] = None` (line 58)
     - **Validation** (lines 155-161): Ensures only one of `json_schema`, `regex`, or `ebnf` is set at a time

**3. /absolute/path/to/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py** (Request Handler)
   - **_build_sampling_params()** method (lines 127-169): Converts CompletionRequest to SamplingParams dict
     - Maps request fields to sampling_params dictionary:
       - `json_schema: request.json_schema` (line 144)
       - `regex: request.regex` (line 143)
       - `ebnf: request.ebnf` (line 145)
       - Handles response_format for json_schema and structural_tag (lines 156-168)

**4. /absolute/path/to/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_chat.py** (Chat Request Handler)
   - Uses `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()` method
   - Also uses `get_json_schema_constraint()` from `sglang.srt.function_call.utils` for tool call constraints

---

### **TEST FILES - Constrained Generation Patterns**

**1. /absolute/path/to/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py** (Test Mixin for JSON Schema)
   - **TestJSONConstrainedMixin class**: Provides test methods for json_schema constrained decoding
   - Usage pattern in `/generate` endpoint:
     ```python
     {
       "text": "prompt",
       "sampling_params": {
         "json_schema": json.dumps(schema_dict),
         "temperature": 0,
         "max_new_tokens": 128,
       }
     }
     ```
   - Also tests OpenAI client with `response_format={"type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...}}`

**2. /absolute/path/to/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py** (Test Mixin for Regex)
   - **TestRegexConstrainedMixin class**: Provides test methods for regex constrained decoding
   - Usage pattern:
     ```python
     {
       "text": "prompt",
       "sampling_params": {
         "regex": r"^pattern$",
         "temperature": 0,
         "max_new_tokens": 128,
       }
     }
     ```

**3. /absolute/path/to/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py** (Test Mixin for EBNF)
   - **TestEBNFConstrainedMixin class**: Provides test methods for EBNF constrained decoding
   - Usage pattern:
     ```python
     {
       "text": "prompt",
       "sampling_params": {
         "ebnf": 'root ::= "test"',
         "temperature": 0,
         "max_new_tokens": 128,
       }
     }
     ```

**4. /absolute/path/to/test/registered/openai_server/features/test_openai_server_ebnf.py**
   - Integration tests for EBNF constrained decoding via OpenAI API
   - Uses `extra_body={"ebnf": ebnf_grammar}` with openai.Client

**5. /absolute/path/to/test/registered/openai_server/features/test_json_mode.py**
   - Integration tests for JSON mode (`response_format={"type": "json_object"}`)
   - Also tests JSON schema via `response_format={"type": "json_schema", ...}`

**6. /absolute/path/to/test/manual/openai_server/features/test_structural_tag.py**
   - Tests for structural_tag response format
   - Usage: `response_format={"type": "structural_tag", "format": {...}}`

**7. /absolute/path/to/test/registered/openai_server/basic/test_protocol.py**
   - Unit tests for protocol models including request validation
   - Tests field parsing and serialization

**8. /absolute/path/to/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py**
   - Tests for JSON schema constraint generation for tool calls
   - Uses `get_json_schema_constraint()` utility function

**9. /absolute/path/to/test/registered/openai_server/basic/test_serving_completions.py**
   - Tests for request to sampling_params conversion
   - Tests: `test_response_format_json_object()`, `test_response_format_json_schema()`

---

### **KEY MAPPING SUMMARY**

| Parameter | HTTP Request Location | SamplingParams Field | Request Classes |
|-----------|----------------------|---------------------|-----------------|
| **json_schema** | `CompletionRequest.json_schema` OR `response_format.json_schema` | `SamplingParams.json_schema` | Both CompletionRequest & ChatCompletionRequest |
| **regex** | `CompletionRequest.regex` OR `ChatCompletionRequest.regex` | `SamplingParams.regex` | Both |
| **ebnf** | `CompletionRequest.ebnf` OR `ChatCompletionRequest.ebnf` | `SamplingParams.ebnf` | Both |
| **structural_tag** | `response_format.type == "structural_tag"` | `SamplingParams.structural_tag` | Both (via response_format) |

---

### **CONVERSION FLOW**

1. **HTTP Request** arrives with constrained decoding parameters
2. **Protocol Model** (CompletionRequest/ChatCompletionRequest) validates and parses
3. **Conversion Method** (to_sampling_params() or _build_sampling_params()) transforms to dict
4. **SamplingParams** class instantiated with converted values
5. **Validation** ensures mutual exclusivity of constraint types
6. **Sampling** engine uses parameters for constrained decoding

All absolute file paths are provided above for your reference.

### **CONVERSION FLOW**

1. **HTTP Request** arrives with constrained decoding parameters
2. **Protocol Model** (CompletionRequest/ChatCompletionRequest) validates and parses
3. **Conversion Method** (to_sampling_params() or _build_sampling_params()) transforms to dict
4. **SamplingParams** class instantiated with converted values
5. **Validation** ensures mutual exclusivity of constraint types
6. **Sampling** engine uses parameters for constrained decoding

All absolute file paths are provided above for your reference.

class ChatCompletionRequest(BaseModel):
    # Ordered by official OpenAI API documentation
    # https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create
    messages: List[ChatCompletionMessageParam]
    model: str = Field(
        default=DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME,
        description="Model name. Supports LoRA adapters via 'base-model:adapter-name' syntax.",
    )
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None
    logprobs: bool = False
    top_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = Field(
        default=None,
        deprecated="max_tokens is deprecated in favor of the max_completion_tokens field",
        description="The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion. ",
    )
    max_completion_tokens: Optional[int] = Field(
        default=None,
        description="The maximum number of completion tokens for a chat completion request, "
        "including visible output tokens and reasoning tokens. Input tokens are not included. ",
    )
    n: int = 1
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    response_format: Optional[Union[ResponseFormat, StructuralTagResponseFormat]] = None
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None
    stream: bool = False
    stream_options: Optional[StreamOptions] = None
    temperature: Optional[float] = None
    top_p: Optional[float] = None
    user: Optional[str] = None
    tools: Optional[List[Tool]] = Field(default=None, examples=[None])
    tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["auto", "required", "none"]] = Field(
        default="auto", examples=["none"]
    )  # noqa
    return_hidden_states: bool = False
    return_routed_experts: bool = False
    return_cached_tokens_details: bool = False
    reasoning_effort: Optional[Literal["low", "medium", "high"]] = Field(
        default="medium",
        description="Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. "
        "'low' is the least effort, 'high' is the most effort. Reducing reasoning effort can "
        "result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response. "
        "Currently only supported for OpenAI models in the harmony path, i.e GPT-OSS models.",
    )

    # Extra parameters for SRT backend only and will be ignored by OpenAI models.
    top_k: Optional[int] = None
    min_p: Optional[float] = None
    min_tokens: int = 0
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None
    repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None
    no_stop_trim: bool = False
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    continue_final_message: bool = False
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    lora_path: Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], Optional[str]]] = None
    session_params: Optional[Dict] = None
    separate_reasoning: bool = True
    stream_reasoning: bool = True
    chat_template_kwargs: Optional[Dict] = None

    # SGLang multimodal tiling controls (extensions)
    max_dynamic_patch: Optional[int] = None
    min_dynamic_patch: Optional[int] = None

    # Custom logit processor for advanced sampling control
    custom_logit_processor: Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], str]] = None
    custom_params: Optional[Dict] = None

    # For request id
    rid: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Extra key for classifying the request (e.g. cache_salt)
    extra_key: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Cache salt for request caching
    cache_salt: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Priority for the request
    priority: Optional[int] = None

    # For PD disaggregation
    bootstrap_host: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    bootstrap_port: Optional[Union[List[Optional[int]], int]] = None
    bootstrap_room: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None

    # For data parallel rank routing
    data_parallel_rank: Optional[int] = None

    # OpenAI/SGLang default sampling parameters
    _DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = {
        "temperature": 1.0,
        "top_p": 1.0,
        "top_k": -1,
        "min_p": 0.0,
        "repetition_penalty": 1.0,
    }

    @model_validator(mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def set_tool_choice_default(cls, values):
        if values.get("tool_choice") is None:
            if values.get("tools") is None:
                values["tool_choice"] = "none"

response_format["json_schema"] = {
                "name": name_,
                "schema": schema,
                "strict": strict_,

        return values

    def to_sampling_params(
        self,
        stop: List[str],
        model_generation_config: Dict[str, Any],
        tool_call_constraint: Optional[ToolCallConstraint] = None,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Convert request to sampling parameters.
        Priority: user value > model generation_config > OpenAI defaults
        """

        def get_param(param_name: str):
            value = getattr(self, param_name)
            if value is None:
                return model_generation_config.get(
                    param_name, self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS[param_name]
                )
            return value

        # add per user request
        spaces_between_special_tokens = (
            True
            if self.chat_template_kwargs is None
            else self.chat_template_kwargs.get("spaces_between_special_tokens", True)
        )

        sampling_params = {
            "temperature": get_param("temperature"),
            "max_new_tokens": self.max_completion_tokens or self.max_tokens,
            "min_new_tokens": self.min_tokens,
            "stop": stop,
            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
            "top_p": get_param("top_p"),
            "top_k": get_param("top_k"),
            "min_p": get_param("min_p"),
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "repetition_penalty": get_param("repetition_penalty"),
            "regex": self.regex,
            "ebnf": self.ebnf,
            "n": self.n,
            "no_stop_trim": self.no_stop_trim,
            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
            "skip_special_tokens": self.skip_special_tokens,
            "logit_bias": self.logit_bias,
            "custom_params": self.custom_params,
            "sampling_seed": self.seed,
            "spaces_between_special_tokens": spaces_between_special_tokens,
        }

        if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
            sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                self.response_format.json_schema.schema_
            )
        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
            sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
            sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                self.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
            )

        # Check if there are already existing output constraints
        has_existing_constraints = (
            sampling_params.get("regex")
            or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
            or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
            or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
        )

        if tool_call_constraint and has_existing_constraints:
            logger.warning("Constrained decoding is not compatible with tool calls.")
        elif tool_call_constraint:
            constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
            if constraint_type == "structural_tag":
                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                    constraint_value.model_dump(by_alias=True)
                )
            elif constraint_type == "json_schema":
                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
                    constraint_value  # type: ignore
                )
            else:
                sampling_params[constraint_type] = constraint_value

        return sampling_params


class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
    role: Optional[str] = None
    content: Optional[str] = None

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
"""Utilities for converting a JSONPath expression into a JSON Schema.

The generated schema ensures the LLM output is valid JSON containing
the structure implied by the JSONPath pattern.

Supported JSONPath subset
─────────────────────────
  $                          – root object
  .key / ['key']             – child member access
  [*]                        – array wildcard (any index)
  [0], [1], …               – specific numeric index
  ..key                      – recursive descent (approximated)

For example ``$.store.book[*].author`` produces a schema that
requires the output to be a JSON object with a ``store`` property
that contains a ``book`` array whose items each have an ``author``
property.
"""

import json
import logging
import re
from typing import Any, Dict, List

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class JsonPathError(Exception):
    """Raised when a JSONPath expression cannot be parsed."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tokeniser
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(
    r"""
    (\.\.)                # recursive descent
    | \.(\w+)             # dot-notation child
    | \['\s*(\w+)\s*'\]   # bracket-notation child (single quotes)
    | \["\s*(\w+)\s*"\]   # bracket-notation child (double quotes)
    | \[(\*)\]            # array wildcard
    | \[(\d+)\]           # numeric index
    """,
    re.VERBOSE,
)


def _tokenize(path: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Return a list of path segment dicts from a JSONPath string.

    Each dict has a ``"type"`` key (``"child"``, ``"array_wildcard"``,
    ``"index"``, or ``"recursive"``) and a ``"value"`` key where applicable.
    """
    if not path.startswith("$"):
        raise JsonPathError(
            f"JSONPath must start with '$', got: {path!r}"
        )

    rest = path[1:]  # strip leading '$'
    tokens: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    pos = 0

    while pos < len(rest):
        m = _TOKEN_RE.match(rest, pos)
        if m is None:
            raise JsonPathError(
                f"Cannot parse JSONPath at position {pos + 1}: "
                f"…{rest[pos:pos+20]!r}… in {path!r}"
            )

        if m.group(1):  # recursive descent (..)
            # Peek ahead to grab the field name after '..'
            inner = _TOKEN_RE.match(rest, m.end())
            if inner is None or not (inner.group(2) or inner.group(3) or inner.group(4)):
                raise JsonPathError(
                    f"Expected a field name after '..' at position {m.end() + 1} "
                    f"in {path!r}"
                )
            field = inner.group(2) or inner.group(3) or inner.group(4)
            tokens.append({"type": "recursive", "value": field})
            pos = inner.end()
        elif m.group(2):  # .key
            tokens.append({"type": "child", "value": m.group(2)})
            pos = m.end()
        elif m.group(3):  # ['key']
            tokens.append({"type": "child", "value": m.group(3)})
            pos = m.end()
        elif m.group(4):  # ["key"]
            tokens.append({"type": "child", "value": m.group(4)})
            pos = m.end()
        elif m.group(5):  # [*]
            tokens.append({"type": "array_wildcard"})
            pos = m.end()
        elif m.group(6) is not None:  # [0]
            tokens.append({"type": "index", "value": int(m.group(6))})
            pos = m.end()
        else:
            raise JsonPathError(
                f"Unexpected match at position {pos + 1} in {path!r}"
            )

    return tokens


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Schema builder
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _build_schema(tokens: List[Dict[str, Any]], idx: int = 0) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Recursively build a JSON Schema from *tokens* starting at *idx*.

    Returns a JSON-Schema-compatible dict.
    """
    if idx >= len(tokens):
        # Leaf – accept any JSON value
        return {}

    token = tokens[idx]

    if token["type"] == "child":
        inner = _build_schema(tokens, idx + 1)
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                token["value"]: inner,
            },
            "required": [token["value"]],
        }

    elif token["type"] == "array_wildcard":
        inner = _build_schema(tokens, idx + 1)
        return {
            "type": "array",
            "items": inner,
        }

    elif token["type"] == "index":
        inner = _build_schema(tokens, idx + 1)
        # Model as an array that must contain at least (index+1) items
        # with the element at the given index matching the inner schema.
        min_items = token["value"] + 1
        schema: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "type": "array",
            "minItems": min_items,
        }
        # Use prefixItems to place the constraint at the right position
        if token["value"] == 0:
            schema["items"] = inner
        else:
            prefix: List[Dict[str, Any]] = [{} for _ in range(token["value"])]
            prefix.append(inner)
            schema["prefixItems"] = prefix
        return schema

    elif token["type"] == "recursive":
        # Recursive descent is approximated: we require the field to exist
        # *somewhere* in the structure.  The simplest faithful approximation
        # that still constrains generation is to just require the field at
        # the current level (like a regular child accessor) – this keeps the
        # schema deterministic and usable by grammar backends.
        inner = _build_schema(tokens, idx + 1)
        return {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
                token["value"]: inner,
            },
            "required": [token["value"]],
        }

    raise JsonPathError(f"Unknown token type: {token['type']}")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Public API
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str:
    """Convert a JSONPath expression to a JSON Schema string.

    Parameters
    ----------
    json_path:
        A JSONPath expression such as ``$.store.book[*].author``.

    Returns
    -------
    str
        A JSON string representing the equivalent JSON Schema.

    Raises
    ------
    JsonPathError
        If the expression cannot be parsed.
    """
    json_path = json_path.strip()
    if not json_path:
        raise JsonPathError("Empty JSONPath expression")

    tokens = _tokenize(json_path)

    if not tokens:
        # Just "$" – any valid JSON
        return json.dumps({})

    schema = _build_schema(tokens)
    return json.dumps(schema)


def validate_json_path(json_path: str) -> None:
    """Validate that *json_path* is a parseable JSONPath expression.

    Raises :class:`JsonPathError` on failure.
    """
    json_path_to_json_schema(json_path)  # will raise on bad input

# ==============================================================================
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
except ImportError:
    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SamplingParams:
    """
    The sampling parameters.

    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
    for the documentation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
        self.stop_strs = stop
        if stop_token_ids:
            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
        else:
            self.stop_token_ids = None
        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.top_p = top_p
        self.top_k = top_k
        self.min_p = min_p
        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
        self.regex = regex
        self.n = n
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
        self.custom_params = custom_params
        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed

        # Process some special cases
        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
            self.temperature = 1.0
            self.top_k = 1
        if self.top_k == -1:
            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary

    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
            )
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
            )
        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
                )
            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
                )
        if self.logit_bias is not None:
            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
                        f"{token_id}."
                    )

        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
        # Process stop strings
        if self.stop_strs is None:
            self.stop_strs = []
            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]

            stop_str_max_len = 0
            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
                if tokenizer is not None:
                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
                else:
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len

        # Process stop regex strings
        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
            self.stop_regex_strs = []
            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]

            stop_regex_max_len = 0
            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
                stop_regex_max_len = max(
                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
                )

            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len


# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
# to be buffered to match the input regex string
# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
# token
def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))


MAX_LEN = 2**30


def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
    total = 0
    for token, value in subpattern:
        if token in {
            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
        }:
            total += 1
        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
            # EG: (a\d+) ->
            # [(SUBPATTERN,
            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
            _, branches = value
            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

            raise ValueError(
                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
            )
        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
                )
            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
                )
        if self.logit_bias is not None:
            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
                        f"{token_id}."
                    )

        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
        # Process stop strings
        if self.stop_strs is None:
            self.stop_strs = []
            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]

            stop_str_max_len = 0
            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
                if tokenizer is not None:
                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
                else:
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len

        # Process stop regex strings
        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
            self.stop_regex_strs = []
            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]

            stop_regex_max_len = 0
            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
                stop_regex_max_len = max(
                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
                )

            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len


# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
# to be buffered to match the input regex string
# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
# token
def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))


MAX_LEN = 2**30


def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
    total = 0
    for token, value in subpattern:
        if token in {
            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
        }:
            total += 1
        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
            # EG: (a\d+) ->
            # [(SUBPATTERN,
            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
            _, branches = value
            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from concurrent import futures
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List

import torch

from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
    create_grammar_backend,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class GrammarManager:
    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
        self.scheduler = scheduler
        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
                self.server_args,
                scheduler.tokenizer,
                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
            )
        else:
            self.grammar_backend = None

        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank

        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
        )

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.grammar_queue)

    def clear(self):
        if self.grammar_backend:
            self.grammar_backend.reset()

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
        for req in self.grammar_queue:
            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
                if req.grammar:
                    req.grammar.cancel()
                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")

    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        add_to_grammar_queue = False
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if self.grammar_backend is None:
                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
            else:
                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)

                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                    key, req.require_reasoning
                )
                req.grammar = value

                if not cache_hit:
                    req.grammar_key = key
                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
                else:
                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        if add_to_grammar_queue:
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)

        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """
        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.

        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)

        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
        """
        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()

        # Poll for ready requests
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
                if i in ready_req_idxs:
                    continue

                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
                    continue

                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
                if req.grammar.done():
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)

            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)

        # Check failed requests
        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
                if (
                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
                ):
                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
        else:
            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
                all_gather_output,
                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
            )
            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])

        # Return ready requests
        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)
            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                continue

            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Return failed requests
        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)

            req.grammar.cancel()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
        self.grammar_queue = [
            req
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
        ]
        return return_reqs
